rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WALLY HEDRICK TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) Probate Code section 17005(b) provides that: "If a living trust has no trustee, the proper county for commencement of a proceeding for appointing a trustee is the county where the trust property, or some portion of the trust property, is located." Here, no trust property is located in San Francisco County. Should the proper venue be in Alameda County where some paintings and artwork are located? 2) Should a bond be required as a condition of appointment of Sam Parker as successor trustee? Section 15602(a)(3) provides that a trustee is not required to give a bond, "unless an individual who is not named as a trustee in the trust instrument is appointed as a trustee by the court." Section 15602(b) provides in relevant part that "the court may not excuse the requirement of a bond except under compelling circumstances. For purposes of this section, a request by all the adult beneficiaries of a trust that bond be waived is deemed to constitute a compelling circumstance." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF ROWAN STRUMMER TELLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) If cash alone is over $221,000, $165,000 bond is not sufficient. [CRC 7.204 and 7.207.] (3) The accounting is missing the schedule of non-cash assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period, which should be exactly the assets on hand at the end of the prior accounting. [Probate Code Section 1061(a)(1).] If page 2 of 2 attached behind Schedule F of this accounting is meant to be the non-cash assets on hand at the beginning of this accounting period, it does not reflect either the assets on hand at the end of the prior accounting period or the valuations in the Corrected I&A filed on 8/6/21. (4) In any event, a Corrected I&A is not appropriate at this point (third accounting) of the estate administration. If there are changes in the form of assets or changes in valuations for other reasons, that should be shown in an informational schedule in the accounting, not in a Corrected I&A. (5) There is no total shown at the bottom of page 1 of 2 of Schedule A and page 2 of 2 follows the Schedule of Assets on Hand at Beginning and appears to be part of Schedule A and also reflects dividends from an ETF (which was not shown on hand at the beginning of this accounting period). (6) Why was Miriam Telles reimbursed $23,3546.32 on 3/22/22? (7) What is meant by "reimburse Trefzger account" on 10/20/22? (8) Reimbursements to Miriam Telles must show who the original payee was. (9) Please file something substantiating the losses shown on the unidentified schedule (Other Credits). (10) The Morgan Stanley account is NOT all cash. The statement reflects stocks and an ETF; these must be set forth in detail in the accounting. When were they acquired? (11) There is no liabilities schedule, but the fees awarded on 4/5/22 to Ernest Der, as the minor's guardian ad litem in the minor's father's estate, do not appear to have been paid yet. A fully amended accounting must be filed to address these issues (and to provide schedules in an orderly and logical fashion). Notice must be served after the amended accounting is filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/7/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WARREN H. KING SR
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed: (1) No waivers of bond have been filed. (2) A printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely was not attached to the proof of service of the Notice. (3) The mandatory Judicial Council form Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities was incomplete and only supplemented in a separate pleading. (4) The petitioner is a "domestic partner" but does not state whether she is a registered domestic parther with standing. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their full name. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ELEANOR JUDITH DAVIS SURVIVOR'S TRUST CREATED UNDER THE DAVIS FAMILY REVOCABLE INTER VIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Fourth Account And Report Of Successor Trustee And Petition For Its Settlement; And Petition For Approval Of Trustee'S Construction Of The Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/14/2023. 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) Is Jeffrey McHenry represented by an attorney in the probate proceeding in Santa Clara County? If so, notice must also be given to his attorney. 3) The beginning balance of this account cannot be verified without settlement of the prior account and its ending balance. Is the petitioner requesting an order merely acknowledging the account as presented? 4) Did the trustee exercise its discretion under section 6.1(d)(2) of the governing trust by withholding distribution of any part of net income as unnecessary for the reasonable care, maintenance, support and education of the income beneficiary? If so, please state the total amount of undistributed net income and provide facts supporting the petitioner's necessity determination. 5) Without settlement of the prior account and confirmation that the petitioner made all distribution of net income determined to be necessary for the income beneficiary during the prior account period, the residue distributable to the remainder beneficiary as of the end of this account period cannot be ascertained. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DISHUI LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The former spouse has no standing. If she is to be the personal representative, there is a potential conflict if she were also to be appointed as the guardian ad litem for the minor, which she has not yet been. (2) How old is the minor? (3) A guardianship of the estate will need to be established for him before the estate can be distributed to him. (4) Bond will be required since the minor cannot waive bond, and in any event, bond is required for a non-resident personal representative. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (5) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (6) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/7/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LIONEL WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption fails to disclose that IAEA authority is being requested. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Bond will be required for a non-resident personal representative. [LRSF 14.16 F.] (4) Item 8 of the petition does not state the relationship of each person listed. (5) Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. (6) If the petitioner is the trustee of the devisee trust, all trust beneficiaries must also be listed, clearly identified as such, and must receive notice. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/7/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TATSURO IWAMOTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/20/23. (No earlier availability.) If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Probate Code Sec. 8113.] Courtesy copies of the proof of service of notice to the Japanese consulate must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/8/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
